Question: Changes can be reversible or irreversible.
Melting ice is a reversible change.
Boiling water is a reversible change.
Rusting of iron is an irreversible change.
Dissolving sugar in water is reversible.
Condensation of water vapour is reversible.
Ice changing to water is physical change.
Baking a cake is chemical change.
Dissolving salt in water is reversible.
Iron can rust in presence of water and air.
Mixing oil and water is reversible.
Dissolving sugar in tea is reversible.
Making juice from fruits is irreversible.
